2603.24203.title: Invisible Threats from Model Context Protocol: Generating Stealthy Injection Payload via Tree-based Adaptive Search
2603.24203.paper_summary: Recent advances in the Model Context Protocol (MCP) have enabled large language models (LLMs) to invoke external tools with unprecedented ease. This creates a new class of powerful and tool augmented agents. Unfortunately, this capability also introduces an under explored attack surface, specifically the malicious manipulation of tool responses. Existing techniques for indirect prompt injection that target MCP suffer from high deployment costs, weak semantic coherence, or heavy white box requirements. Furthermore, they are often easily detected by recently proposed defenses. In this paper, we propose Tree structured Injection for Payloads (TIP), a novel black-box attack which generates natural payloads to reliably seize control of MCP enabled agents even under defense. Technically, We cast payload generation as a tree structured search problem and guide the search with an attacker LLM operating under our proposed coarse-to-fine optimization framework. To stabilize learning and avoid local optima, we introduce a path-aware feedback mechanism that surfaces only high quality historical trajectories to the attacker model. The framework is further hardened against defensive transformations by explicitly conditioning the search on observable defense signals and dynamically reallocating the exploration budget. Extensive experiments on four mainstream LLMs show that TIP attains over 95% attack success in undefended settings while requiring an order of magnitude fewer queries than prior adaptive attacks. Against four representative defense approaches, TIP preserves more than 50% effectiveness and significantly outperforms the state-of-the-art attacks. By implementing the attack on real world MCP systems, our results expose an invisible but practical threat vector in MCP deployments. We also discuss potential mitigation approaches to address this critical security gap.
2603.24203.paper_summary_zh: 最近在模型上下文協議（MCP）方面的進展使大型語言模型（LLMs）能夠以前所未有的輕鬆程度調用外部工具。<lb/>這創造了一類強大且增強工具的代理。不幸的是，這一能力也引入了一個尚未充分探索的攻擊面，特別是對工具響應的惡意操控。<lb/>現有針對MCP的間接提示注入技術存在高部署成本、語義一致性差或重白盒要求等問題。<lb/>此外，它們通常容易被最近提出的防禦措施檢測到。<lb/>在本文中，我們提出了針對有效載荷的樹狀注入（TIP），這是一種新穎的黑盒攻擊，能夠生成自然的有效載荷，可靠地控制啟用MCP的代理，即使在防禦下也能做到。<lb/>從技術上講，我們將有效載荷生成視為樹狀搜索問題，並在我們提出的粗到細優化框架下，利用攻擊者LLM引導搜索。<lb/>為了穩定學習並避免局部最優，我們引入了一種路徑感知反饋機制，僅將高質量的歷史軌跡呈現給攻擊者模型。<lb/>該框架進一步加固了對抗防禦轉換的能力，通過明確地將搜索條件化於可觀察的防禦信號並動態重新分配探索預算。<lb/>在四個主流LLM上的廣泛實驗表明，TIP在未防禦的環境中達到了超過95%的攻擊成功率，同時所需查詢數量比先前的自適應攻擊少一個量級。<lb/>面對四種代表性的防禦方法，TIP保持了超過50%的有效性，並顯著超越了最先進的攻擊。<lb/>通過在現實世界的MCP系統上實施攻擊，我們的結果揭示了MCP部署中一個隱形但實際的威脅向量。<lb/>我們還討論了潛在的緩解方法，以解決這一關鍵的安全漏洞。
2603.24203.author: Yulin Shen et.al.
2603.24203.authors: Yulin Shen, Xudong Pan, Geng Hong, Min Yang
